2606.15880.title: Deep Residual Injection for Full-Spectrum Forensic Signal Perception in Multimodal Large Language Models
2606.15880.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have been increasingly adopted in forensics for their robust semantic understanding. As AI-generated images become realistic, semantic-level inconsistencies alone are often insufficient for reliable detection. This motivates a critical question: whether MLLMs can achieve full-spectrum forensic signal perception, i.e., capturing low-level generator artifacts without sacrificing pre-trained semantic knowledge. We further perform a layer-wise analysis of forensic signal perception in MLLMs, showing that semantic information is primarily formed in the early-to-middle layers, whereas direct fine-tuning for artifact learning disrupts these semantic representations. Based on this insight, we propose Deep Visual Residual MLLM (Deep-VRM) to preserve early semantic processing while injecting artifact-specific visual signals as a residual path into an intermediate layer, where they are fused with semantic token representations and propagated through subsequent trainable layers. This enables later layers to jointly model semantic reasoning and signal-level forensic cues, and surprisingly, the model learns to adaptively leverage different levels of forensic signals depending on the input, achieving robust and generalizable detection performance. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art across most benchmarks. The code and data are available at https://github.com/KQL11/Deep-VRM.
2606.15880.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在法醫學中的應用越來越廣泛，因為它們具有強大的語義理解能力。隨著AI生成的圖像變得越來越真實，僅僅依賴語義層面的不一致性往往不足以進行可靠的檢測。這引發了一個關鍵問題：MLLMs是否能夠實現全範圍的法醫信號感知，即在不犧牲預訓練語義知識的情況下捕捉低層次的生成器伪影。我們進一步對MLLMs中的法醫信號感知進行了逐層分析，顯示語義信息主要是在早期到中層形成，而直接針對伪影學習的微調會破壞這些語義表示。基於這一見解，我們提出了深度視覺殘差MLLM（Deep-VRM），以保留早期的語義處理，同時將特定於伪影的視覺信號作為殘差路徑注入到中間層，在那裡它們與語義標記表示融合並通過隨後的可訓練層傳播。這使得後續層能夠共同建模語義推理和信號層面的法醫線索，令人驚訝的是，模型學會根據輸入自適應地利用不同層次的法醫信號，實現了穩健且具可泛化性的檢測性能。大量實驗表明，我們的方法在大多數基準測試中達到了最先進的水平。代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/KQL11/Deep-VRM 獲得。
2606.15880.author: Kaiqing Lin et.al.
2606.15880.authors: Kaiqing Lin, Zhiyuan Yan, Ruoxin Chen, Ke-Yue Zhang, Yue Zhou, Caiyong Piao, Bin Li, Taiping Yao, Bo Wang, Youchang Xiao, Shouhong Ding
